2606.31719.title: Seeing Is Not Sharing: Some Vision-Language Models Overestimate Common Ground in Asymmetric Dialogue
2606.31719.paper_summary: In collaborative dialogue, shared perception does not guarantee shared interpretation. Mutual understanding must be established through interaction. We investigate whether vision-language models (VLMs) can distinguish what could be shared from what has been shared between dialogue participants through grounding. We formulate this as an interpretation-matching task on 13,077 annotated reference expressions from HCRC MapTask dialogues, and evaluate VLMs under systematically controlled manipulations of dialogue context and map-information access. Our results show that providing authentic map images improves overall performance but shifts models toward over-predicting alignment. Textual descriptions of the same map content reproduce this bias, while non-informative images suppress alignment predictions entirely, indicating that the bias is driven by task-relevant map content, not the visual channel. This improvement comes at the cost of degraded accuracy on non-aligned cases. Calibration analysis and reference-chain tracking further suggest that models rely on static referential cues on the maps rather than tracking how grounding unfolds through dialogue history. We observe these patterns most clearly in Qwen3-VL-8B-Instruct and, to varying degrees, in four additional models from two architecture families. In models that exhibit the bias, map content, whether presented visually or textually, is treated as evidence of mutual understanding, conflating potential with established common ground.
2606.31719.paper_summary_zh: 在協作對話中，共享的感知並不保證共享的詮釋。<lb/>必須通過互動建立相互理解。<lb/>我們調查視覺-語言模型（VLMs）是否能夠區分對話參與者之間可能共享的內容與實際共享的內容，這是通過基礎來實現的。<lb/>我們將這一問題表述為一個詮釋匹配任務，基於來自HCRC MapTask對話的13,077個註釋參考表達，並在系統控制的對話上下文和地圖信息訪問的操控下評估VLMs。<lb/>我們的結果顯示，提供真實的地圖圖像能改善整體表現，但使模型傾向於過度預測對齊。<lb/>相同地圖內容的文本描述重現了這一偏見，而非信息性圖像則完全抑制了對齊預測，這表明該偏見是由任務相關的地圖內容驅動的，而不是視覺通道。<lb/>這一改進以非對齊情況的準確性下降為代價。<lb/>校準分析和參考鏈追蹤進一步表明，模型依賴於地圖上的靜態參考線索，而不是追蹤基礎如何通過對話歷史展開。<lb/>我們在Qwen3-VL-8B-Instruct中最清楚地觀察到這些模式，並在來自兩個架構系列的四個附加模型中以不同程度出現。<lb/>在表現出偏見的模型中，無論是以視覺還是文本形式呈現的地圖內容，都被視為相互理解的證據，將潛在的共享基礎與已建立的共同基礎混淆。
2606.31719.author: Nan Li et.al.
2606.31719.authors: Nan Li, Albert Gatt, Massimo Poesio
